steps.text: Report suspected Zika cases to public health departments.
Report confirmed Zika cases to CDC.
Distribute traps to collect Ae. aegypti mosquitoes.
Apply insecticides (larvicides and adulticides) by backpack and truck-mounted spraying.
Alert hospitals and clinicians about the threat, and offer guidance for informing patients at risk.
Conduct a thorough review of patient charts and extensive interviews.
Make recommendations about how to detect adverse events during dialysis.
Use the new yellow fever test kit to yield results in less than 4 hours.
Take detailed travel histories from patients with febrile disease.
Determine the types of care that require standard versus extra infection control precautions.

preconditions.text: Connecticut Department of Public Health requests help from NCEZID to help with the investigation.

constraints.text: Postpone travel to areas with Zika if pregnant.
Apply recommended personal protective equipment and quarantine measures.
